title: Funding squeeze unwind signal
hypothesis: When funding is extreme, open interest has recently expanded and price no longer confirms the crowded direction, subsequent OI contraction or funding normalization may mark a squeeze-unwind window. The first implementation should generate alerts and block late crowded entries rather than automatically taking contrarian trades.

inputs: funding rates aligned to funding intervals
open interest series with fixed venue definition
mark price or perp candles for price continuation and stall detection
trade-derived volume for liquidity sanity checks
entry_rules: abs(funding_rate) is above configured rolling percentile or absolute threshold
open interest increased during the prior confirmation window
price continuation in the crowded direction stalls or reverses below threshold
trade volume confirms the window is not illiquid noise
signal is emitted as alert or filter by default
exit_rules: open interest contraction completes or reverses
price breaks out again in the crowded direction
manual review rejects current venue data quality

test_plan: Use BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT and SOLUSDT perps with fixed exchange-specific OI definitions.
Label funding percentile, OI change and price continuation over 15m, 1h and funding-interval windows.
Compare late-entry filter behavior for oi_confirmed_momentum and funding_carry_vol_filter candidates.
Measure forward returns after crowded continuation, stall and unwind states separately.
Apply conservative fee and slippage assumptions to any simulated contrarian entry variant.
Keep standalone execution disabled unless replay proves stability across multiple symbols and months.
risk_controls: Default output is alert or late-entry block, not automatic contrarian entry.
Do not mix OI definitions across exchanges without explicit venue mapping.
Disable signal during exchange outage, abnormal funding calculation or stale OI data.
No high leverage, no martingale and no repeated entries during squeeze windows.
No production config changes or live trading actions.
